description_long: This is a small, single-story brick house with a gable roof covered in corrugated gray sheets. The front facade features three rectangular windows, one of which is slightly smaller and offset, framed by white trim. The brickwork is primarily light-colored with decorative orange brick accents at the corners and beneath the windows. A single, rusted metal chimney protrudes from the roof near one end, indicating a likely wood or coal heating system. The side and rear walls are plain, with no visible windows or doors, and the base of the house shows some weathering and dirt accumulation. The overall appearance suggests a modest, utilitarian residential structure typical of Eastern European or post-Soviet urban neighborhoods.
description: A small, single-story brick house with a gable roof, three front windows, and a rusted metal chimney. Decorative orange brick accents and weathered surfaces give it a utilitarian, lived-in look.
description_view_0: Front view showing three windows with white trim and orange brick accents, plus the gable roof and chimney visible behind.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a plain brick wall with no windows or doors, and the corrugated roof with the chimney just visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, but without windows or decorative brickwork; the chimney is more prominent from this angle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing another plain brick wall and the corrugated roof, with the chimney base clearly visible.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple entryway with no visible porch or steps, likely a single door aligned with the window arrangement, minimal decorative elements.
